# Paper Outline

## Title options

1. Manufactured Traction: Signal Corruption in Digital Markets
2. When Proof Becomes Synthetic: The Ethics of Manufactured Growth
3. The Architecture of Manufactured Traction
4. From Fake Engagement to Signal Corruption

## Structure

1. Introduction: digital markets allocate trust through proxy signals.
2. Definition: manufactured traction as signal corruption.
3. Theory: manufactured attention, recommender systems, social proof, dark patterns.
4. Five signal layers: visibility, credibility, conversion, attribution, legitimacy.
5. Boundary: legitimate growth vs manipulative growth.
6. Visibility corruption: clipping, trend simulation, Reddit astroturfing, engagement pods.
7. Credibility corruption: fake reviews, hidden sponsorship, testimonial editing, local reviews.
8. Conversion corruption: fake leads, invalid traffic, dark patterns, fake installs.
9. Attribution corruption: click flooding, install hijacking, SDK spoofing, cookie stuffing.
10. Legitimacy corruption: Product Hunt, GitHub stars, ARR inflation, compliance theater.
11. Synthetic content: AI music, streaming fraud, synthetic influencers.
12. Detection: red flags, network coordination, source audit, evidence ladder.
13. Governance: FTC, ACCC, platform policies, limits of enforcement.
14. Harms: consumers, creators, competitors, investors, platforms.
15. Ethical alternative: transparent growth and real proof.
16. Conclusion: markets fail when proof becomes synthetic.
